response.justification: Bogdan is a seasoned full-stack and web engineer with genuine breadth across TypeScript, Node.js, and distributed service work, but he does not meet the core minimum qualifications for this role. The job requires 8 years of C++ experience; his resume lists roughly 6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments plus a bachelor thesis project, and C++ does not even appear in his skills section. He also lacks the 5-year large-scale infrastructure/cloud compute platform background and has no formal technical leadership or people-mentoring history at the required level. His experience is firmly in application-layer web and tooling development rather than cloud platform or systems programming.
strengths: Strong TDD and software craftsmanship culture with concrete evidence (testing libraries, workshops, trunk-based development advocacy)
Exposure to high-throughput distributed service work at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, low-latency design)
Broad polyglot background and willingness to pick the right tool, including Kubernetes, AWS CDK, and agentic/AI infrastructure work
concerns: Virtually no C++ experience — roughly 6 months at National Instruments and a thesis project versus the 8-year minimum requirement
No formal technical leadership role; workshop facilitation and mentoring sessions do not substitute for 2+ years leading cloud or infrastructure engineers
Career trajectory is web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) with no demonstrated cloud compute platform, storage, or hardware architecture depth
